title: flakey/broken detection of criu version
body:
Bizarre new flake:
```
[It] podman checkpoint latest running container
...
# podman-remote [options] container checkpoint second
Error: checkpoint/restore requires at least CRIU 31100
```
All other checkpoint/restore tests pass on that same task, so obviously this is a false error.

Only one instance in my logs: [f37 remote](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6079676305637376/html/int-remote-fedora-37-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-latest-running-container--1)

First step, I would suggest, might be to instrument `libpod/container_internal_common.go:checkpointRestoreSupported()` so it emits the criu version it thinks it's finding.

comments.body: Likely a underlying error in the rpc protocol between podman and criu, I think the first step is to return the original error and not consider an error as minimum version not matched.

Here we go! [f38 remote](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5535861907914752/html/int-remote-fedora-38-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-a-container-started-with-rm--1):
```
# podman-remote [options] container checkpoint ..... 
Error: failed to check for criu version: write criu-xprt-cln: broken pipe
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I assume we never saw it again and close it

[Last week, actually](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6728335036776448/html/int-remote-fedora-39-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-and-restore-containers-with-print-stats--1):
```
Error: failed to check for criu version: write criu-xprt-cln: broken pipe
```

Today in system tests, [rawhide remote](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6126978862940160/html/sys-remote-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00633):
```
<+024ms> # # podman-remote container checkpoint 53b2c1ff91face3d7788b502eec0415a74c752347d6d8cdc280a165a5b192b3c
<+461ms> # 53b2c1ff91face3d7788b502eec0415a74c752347d6d8cdc280a165a5b192b3c
         #
<+014ms> # # podman-remote container restore --ignore-static-ip --ignore-static-mac 53b2c1ff91face3d7788b502eec0415a74c752347d6d8cdc280a165a5b192b3c
         # [03:39:16.939415637]
         # Error: failed to check for criu version: write criu-xprt-cln: broken pipe
```

As far as flakes go, this is a pretty sweet & mellow one, infrequent, easily recognized & categorized. [one more, f40](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6577273988448256/html/int-remote-fedora-40-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-container-with-export--migration---1)

```
Error: failed to check for criu version: write criu-xprt-cln: broken pipe
criu swrk failed: exit status 255
```

Not much more helpful I fear

https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6055388473196544/html/int-podman-fedora-40-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-container-with-export-and-verify-non-default-runtime--1


--- 

Getting the actual stderr from criu would be useful but seems to be impossible to implement correctly
https://github.com/checkpoint-restore/go-criu/pull/175#issuecomment-2196544369

This is still failing
https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5930875856093184/html/int-remote-debian-13-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-checkpoint-podman-checkpoint-and-restore-container-out-of-and-into-pod--ipc-net-uts-pid---1
```
Error: restoring containers into pod: failed to check for criu version: write criu-xprt-cln: broken pipe
criu swrk failed: exit status 255
```

@adrianreber @rst0git Any reason why the version check keeps failing randomly? How can we get proper output capture of criu to know what is wrong, ref https://github.com/checkpoint-restore/go-criu/pull/175
